title: Xtra Rich Canadian Beef Pie
description: A rich and indulgent Canadian dish featuring smoked paprika and flaky crust, perfect for a special occasion
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large pan, cook the ground beef over medium high heat until browned, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper to the pan and stir to combine.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour and cold unsalted butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
Gradually add the ice cold water to the bowl, stirring with a fork until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out into a large circle, about 1/4 inch thick.
Spoon the beef mixture into the center of the dough circle, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Brush the edges of the dough with the beaten egg for a golden glaze.
Fold the dough up over the filling, pressing the edges to seal, and use a knife to cut a few slits in the top crust.
Place the pie on a baking sheet and bake for 25 minutes, then flambe with cognac and continue baking for an additional 10 minutes.
Remove the pie from the oven and let it cool for 10 minutes before serving.
